1: Whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.
2: In whom old age was perished?
3: Fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.
4: And juniper roots for for their meat.
5: (They cried after them as after after a thief;)
6: in in caves of the earth, and in in the rocks.
7: Under the nettles they were gathered together.
8: They were viler than the earth.
10: And spare not to spit in my face.
11: They have also let loose the bridle before me.
12: And they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.
14: In the desolation they rolled themselves upon me upon me .
15: And my welfare passeth away as a cloud.
16: The days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
17: And my sinews take no rest.
18: It bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.
19: And I am become like dust and ashes.
20: I stand up, and thou regardest me not not .
21: With thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.
23: And to to the house appointed for all living.
24: Though they cry in his destruction.
25: Was not not my soul grieved for the poor?
26: And when I waited for light, there came darkness.
27: The days of affliction prevented me.
28: I stood up, and and I cried in the congregation.
30: And my bones are burned with heat.
31: And my organ into the voice of them that weep.
